The **2015 [Russian Figure Skating Championships](/wiki/Russian_Figure_Skating_Championships "Russian Figure Skating Championships")** ([Russian](/wiki/Russian_language "Russian language"): Чемпионат России по фигурному катанию на коньках 2015) was held from 24 to 28 December 2014 in [Sochi](/wiki/Sochi "Sochi"). Medals were awarded in the disciplines of [men's singles, ladies' singles](/wiki/Single_skating "Single skating"), [pair skating](/wiki/Pair_skating "Pair skating"), and [ice dancing](/wiki/Ice_dancing "Ice dancing"). The results were among the criteria used to select Russia's teams sent to the [2015 World Championships](/wiki/2015_World_Figure_Skating_Championships "2015 World Figure Skating Championships") and [2015 European Championships](/wiki/2015_European_Figure_Skating_Championships "2015 European Figure Skating Championships").

## Senior Championships

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=2015_Russian_Figure_Skating_Championships&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Senior Championships")]

The senior Championships was held in [Sochi](/wiki/Sochi "Sochi") for the third year in a row. Competitors qualified through international success or by competing in the Russian Cup series' senior-level events.[[1]](#cite_note-calendar-1) 2014 national champions [Adelina Sotnikova](/wiki/Adelina_Sotnikova "Adelina Sotnikova") and [Ekaterina Bobrova](/wiki/Ekaterina_Bobrova "Ekaterina Bobrova") / [Dmitri Soloviev](/wiki/Dmitri_Soloviev "Dmitri Soloviev") were absent from the entry list, which featured 18 men, 18 ladies, 11 pairs, and 10 dance teams.[[2]](#cite_note-entries-2)

## Junior Championships

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=2015_Russian_Figure_Skating_Championships&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Junior Championships")]

The 2015 [Russian Junior Championships](/wiki/Russian_Junior_Figure_Skating_Championships "Russian Junior Figure Skating Championships") ([Russian](/wiki/Russian_language "Russian language"): Первенство России) were held in [Yoshkar-Ola](/wiki/Yoshkar-Ola "Yoshkar-Ola") from 4 to 7 February 2015.[[1]](#cite_note-calendar-1) Competitors qualified by competing in the Russian Cup series' junior-level events. There were 18 qualifiers in the men's event, 18 ladies, 12 pairs, and 15 ice dancing teams.[[8]](#cite_note-Entries-Junior-8) The results of the February competition was part of the selection criteria for the [2015 World Junior Championships](/wiki/2015_World_Junior_Figure_Skating_Championships "2015 World Junior Figure Skating Championships").
